Title: Maggi, Kemas back women entrepreneurs
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Maggi Malaysia has partnered with the Community Development Department (Kemas) to expand its women-focused entrepreneurship programme, particularly in rural and underserved communities.
The Nestlé Malaysia food brand said the collaboration will see the implementation of the Maggi Wanita Cukup Berani (WCB) programme through Kemas' grassroots network.
As part of the WCB 4.0 programme, women are invited to submit business ideas by June 30.
Five selected participants will receive RM5,000 each in seed funding, along with eight weeks of mentorship to help them refine and launch their business concepts.
The initiative was recently introduced at Pusat Kegiatan Masyarakat in Bandar Baru Klang through a community programme organised by Kemas and supported by TV3's 'Wanita Hari Ini' (WHI).
Maggi Malaysia and Singapore business executive officer Ivy Tan Link Cheh said the programme aims to equip women with entrepreneurial skills while leveraging growing opportunities in the digital economy.
"As the digital economy continues to grow, women who begin now can build sustainable income streams, gain confidence and create brighter futures for themselves and their families.
"Women have always been the backbone of families and communities and are increasingly becoming a key pillar of the economy.
"Through Kemas, alongside our collaborations with Google, TikTok, and media partners such as TV3's 'Wanita Hari Ini', we are providing not just inspiration but also practical tools, mentorship and opportunities for women to grow and succeed."
Since its launch, the programme has evolved from a culinary skills initiative into a broader entrepreneurship platform incorporating business fundamentals, digital marketing and financial management.
Maggi aims to reach 10,000 women through the programme by the end of 2026.
Kemas director-general Datuk Mohd Hanafiah Man said the agency remains committed to creating sustainable pathways for women to improve their economic prospects.
"Participation in the programme is not only about presenting business ideas but also about taking the first step towards building a better future."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Wisma Putra: Anwar's visit to Japan to open new avenues of cooperation
Article:
TOKYO: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim will undertake an official visit to Japan from tomorrow until June 10 at the invitation of the Japanese government to strengthen strategic ties and explore new avenues of cooperation between the two countries.
He will be accompanied by Foreign Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan, Investment, Trade and Industry Minister Datuk Seri Johari Abdul Ghani, Entrepreneur and Cooperatives Minister Steven Sim, and senior government officials.
Wisma Putra, in a statement, said the prime minister will be accorded an official welcoming ceremony, followed by a bilateral meeting with his counterpart, Sanae Takaichi, Prime Minister of Japan.
"This meeting will open up opportunities for both leaders to review the progress of bilateral relations and chart new areas of cooperation following the elevation of Malaysia-Japan ties to a Comprehensive Strategic Partnership in December 2023.
"Key areas of discussion are expected to include green technology, energy resilience and transition, environmental cooperation, defence and regional security, as well as higher education collaboration," Wisma Putra said in a statement.
Both leaders are also expected to exchange views on regional and international developments of mutual interest.
Apart from the bilateral meeting, Anwar is also scheduled to deliver a special lecture at the University of Tokyo and a keynote address at the 31st Nikkei Forum: Future of Asia.
Other regional leaders and influential figures scheduled to attend the conference include including Timor-Leste President Jose Ramos-Horta, Laos Prime Minister Sonexay Siphandone, Thailand's Deputy Prime Minister Sihasak Phuangketkeow, and Vietnam's Deputy Prime Minister Le Tien Chau.
Wisma Putra said Anwar's visit will also focus on drawing high-quality investments, with the prime minister scheduled to attend a roundtable session with Japanese industry leaders to explore new investment opportunities in Malaysia.
"Japan remains among Malaysia's most important economic partners and is its fifth-largest trading partner globally since 2024," it said.
In 2025, total Malaysia-Japan bilateral trade amounted to RM142.96 billion. As of December 2025, a total of 2,872 manufacturing projects involving Japanese participation had been implemented in Malaysia.
Collectively, these projects represented investments worth RM107.9 billion and created 347,346 job opportunities.
Japanese Chief Cabinet Secretary Minoru Kihara was earlier quoted by broadcaster NHK World as saying that Anwar and Takaichi are expected to discuss a range of bilateral and international issues.
These include developments in the Middle East, as well as cooperation in security and economic matters.
Kihara said Japan and Malaysia had developed strong ties as comprehensive strategic partners that share common values and principles.
He added that relations with Malaysia were important to advancing Japan's vision of a Free and Open Indo-Pacific.
Anwar's last visit to Japan was in May 2024 to attend the 29th International Conference on the Future of Asia organised by Nikkei.
The visit secured RM1.45 billion in new potential investments as well as RM550 million in potential exports from seven major Japanese companies, particularly in the electrical and electronics, robotics, chemicals and petrochemicals, digital economy, renewable energy and green technology sectors.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: OpenAI plans ChatGPT 'superapp' overhaul ahead of listing
Article:
NEW YORK: OpenAI is planning its biggest ChatGPT overhaul yet, aiming to turn it into a "superapp" with coding tools and AI agents to boost revenue ahead of a potential stock market listing, the Financial Times reported on Sunday.
The changes are part of a broader reorganisation at OpenAI, as it shifts resources to target lucrative enterprise clients and intensify competition with rival Anthropic, the report said, citing more than a dozen current and former employees.
Reuters could not immediately verify the report. OpenAI did not immediately respond to Reuters' request for comment.
The overhaul will give greater prominence and resources to OpenAI's coding product Codex and is set to roll out in the coming weeks, initially appearing as updates to ChatGPT's website and mobile apps, the FT said.
To drive uptake, OpenAI is redesigning ChatGPT's interface with new prompts and features steering users towards coding tools, image generation and partner services such as Canva and Booking.com, the report added.
Most Codex users are paying customers, while 2 million businesses account for about 40 per cent of OpenAI's revenue, the FT said, adding that the company expects that share to rise to 50 per cent by year-end.
ChatGPT serves more than 900 million weekly active users, OpenAI said earlier this year, adding that it had surpassed 50 million consumer subscribers.
Reuters reported in May that OpenAI was preparing a confidential US initial public offering filing in the coming weeks.
However, chief executive officer Sam Altman has said the company is not focused on timing and will go public when it makes sense.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: IATA sees no near-term airline consolidation despite fuel price pressures
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The International Air Transport Association (IATA) does not expect a wave of airline consolidation in Asia Pacific in the near term despite rising fuel costs.
Carriers remain financially resilient and continue to adapt to changing market conditions, it added.
IATA regional vice president for Asia Pacific Sheldon Hee said airlines in the region are particularly vulnerable to fuel price volatility.
But the industry is currently in a relatively strong position following several years of recovery and improved financial performance.
"It goes without saying that airlines in the region are particularly exposed to fuel costs. Asia Pacific carriers are generally more dependent on fuel and are therefore more affected by fuel price volatility."
"We can certainly see evidence of pressure on airlines," he said during a media briefing at IATA's 82nd annual general meeting and World Air Transport Summit 2026.
However, Hee said higher fuel costs alone were unlikely to trigger significant structural changes across the industry in the immediate future.
"Are we likely to see consolidation in the near term? The airline industry has had a couple of relatively good years and Asia Pacific carriers have not been an exception.
"As a result, I believe the industry is generally in a good position to weather the current situation," he said.
Instead of pursuing mergers or consolidation, airlines are adjusting their operating strategies to manage higher costs.
"The first lever airlines can use when faced with cost escalation is to adjust their operating strategy. We are already seeing airlines around the region make adjustments to capacity, particularly within domestic and regional markets."
"Long-haul markets, however, continue to be supported by strong demand. As a result, we are seeing fewer adjustments on long-haul networks," he said.
Hee said the outlook could become more uncertain if geopolitical tensions persist and fuel prices continue to climb, although recent movements in jet fuel prices have provided some relief.
"If the current crisis persists and fuel prices continue to rise, the longer-term outlook becomes more uncertain. It would be difficult to make a reliable prediction at this stage. That said, there are some positive signs."
"Jet fuel prices reached a peak of around US$218 to US$220 per barrel and have since moderated. More recently, prices have generally been in the range of approximately US$150 to US$160 per barrel, although volatility remains," he said.
He added that airlines have repeatedly demonstrated their ability to adapt to market disruptions.
"Airlines have demonstrated that they are capable of adapting to changing market conditions. Therefore, in the near term, we do not expect to see significant structural changes across the industry," he said.
Hee also said IATA remains confident about Asia Pacific's long-term growth prospects despite geopolitical risks, regulatory changes and economic uncertainties.
He said the association continues to project annual passenger growth of between three and four per cent across the region over the next 10 to 20 years.
"Fundamentally, we believe the long-term forecast remains relatively stable. When we talk about a 10, 15 or 20-year horizon, we continue to expect annual growth of around three to four per cent across Asia-Pacific.
"There may be incremental adjustments, but the overall trajectory remains strong," he said.
He added that traffic growth could be affected by geopolitical developments, economic cycles and health crises, but said such disruptions tend to be temporary.
"There will always be short-term fluctuations. At the same time, disruptions can also create opportunities," he said.
He said the ongoing challenges in the Middle East had created operational bottlenecks in the region but also opened opportunities for Asia Pacific hubs to attract more traffic between Asia and Europe.
"For example, the current challenges and conflicts in the Middle East have created bottlenecks in that region. However, they have also created opportunities for Asia Pacific hubs to capture additional traffic flows between Asia and Europe. The market tends to rebalance itself over time," he said.
Within Southeast Asia specifically, Hee said the long-term growth outlook remains positive.
"Within Southeast Asia specifically, we remain confident about the long-term growth trajectory," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Housing Ministry allocates RM267mil to boost Fire and Rescue Department readiness
Article:
TELUK INTAN: The Housing and Local Government Ministry has allocated substantial funding to ensure the Fire and Rescue Department remains fully equipped and on constant standby for emergencies.
Minister Nga Kor Ming said the ministry has approved RM267 million this year to upgrade equipment for search and rescue operations.
"Under the 12th Malaysia Plan, we have also approved RM1.3 billion for the department to acquire critical assets, because we never know when a disaster will strike," he said.
"For that reason, the department has been instructed to remain on 100 per cent standby at all times," he told reporters today after a briefing on the Sungai Perak Phase 1 Integrated River Basin Development Project and related works in Hilir Perak.
He was responding to questions on strengthening the department's search and rescue capabilities, following the case of hiker Jaslinda Saludin, 49, who was found safe near Kampung Lubuk Gaharu in Tapah yesterday.
Nga said the ministry would provide written details on the estimated cost of the search and rescue operation.
"What matters most is that she was found. That is our priority. Of course, every mission has its weaknesses. We will identify them and make improvements," he said, referring to communication issues encountered during the operation.
Jaslinda was part of a climbing expedition involving 13 others and two Forestry Department mountain guides along the Trans Spencer Chapman trail.
The group began their ascent at 2am on May 23 from Pos Gedung, passing Gunung Bah Gading and Gunung Batu Putih towards Kuala Woh.
She and another climber, Mohd Hanafi Neikmad, 41, reportedly stopped due to health issues. However, Jaslinda, a supply chain executive, is believed to have continued towards the peak and was later reported missing after last being seen on May 24.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Asia Pacific to drive 41pct of global air travel growth but challenges remain: IATA
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Asia Pacific's aviation sector is expected to contribute 41 per cent of global passenger growth over the next two decades.
But infrastructure constraints, taxation and increasing regulatory burdens could undermine the region's long-term potential, the International Air Transport Association (IATA) said.
IATA regional vice president for Asia Pacific Sheldon Hee said the region is projected to add 2.4 billion passengers between 2024 and 2044 with passenger numbers rising from 1.7 billion to 4.1 billion over the period.
The region is forecast to grow at a compound annual growth rate of 3.8 per cent.
Despite the strong demand outlook, Hee said infrastructure, taxation, over-regulation and sustainable growth as the key challenges facing the region.
"Despite the challenges on the horizon, we think the demand story remains very strong and will continue to support growth over the next two decades," he said during a media briefing at the IATA 82nd annual general meeting (AGM) and World Air Transport Summit 2026.
One of the biggest concerns is airport capacity.
IATA estimates that more than half of the US$2.4 trillion in airport capital expenditure expected globally by 2040 will be invested in Asia Pacific as governments and airport operators seek to address congestion and capacity constraints.
There are also several major airport development projects expected to handle more than 100 million passengers annually. These include Singapore's Changi Airport and India's Delhi Airport.
In addition, greenfield developments such as New Manila International Airport in the Philippines and Long Thanh International Airport in Vietnam are projected to achieve or surpass the 100 million passengers per annum threshold.
Hee said IATA's analysis showed that about 88 per cent of airports in the region could face capacity constraints by the 2040s, making infrastructure development a continuing challenge.
He said better utilisation of existing assets, technology adoption and digitalisation would be critical to improving airport efficiency.
"Technology and digitalisation will be key to maximising the available capacity," he said, citing initiatives such as IATA's One ID programme, which uses digital identity and biometric technology to streamline passenger processing.
Hee also raised concerns over airport charges and economic regulation across the region.
He said weak regulatory oversight, cross-subsidisation between airports and poorly designed concession agreements could result in higher costs for airlines and passengers.
He said governments have a critical role to play in ensuring sufficient airport capacity, effective economic oversight and long-term infrastructure planning, while remaining responsive to industry concerns regarding airports, air navigation services and airspace constraints.
On taxation, Hee said governments should avoid imposing taxes that increase the cost of air travel and reduce connectivity.
He cited Sweden's decision to abolish its aviation tax in 2025 after the measure, introduced in 2018, contributed to the loss of routes and weakened competitiveness.
"Governments that depart from ICAO's taxation principles often end up doing more harm than good," he said.
According to IATA, aviation contributes 2.5 per cent of Asia Pacific's gross domestic product (GDP) and generates US$3.70 in economic activity elsewhere in the economy for every US$1 of value added by the sector.
Hee cautioned governments against introducing consumer protection regulations that increase costs and complexity without improving outcomes.
"Our view is that governments should look at service delivery from a systemic perspective."
He said delays and disruptions are often caused by multiple factors, including airport congestion and airspace constraints, rather than airlines alone.
On sustainability, Hee said Asia Pacific has significant potential to become a major producer of sustainable aviation fuel (SAF) with around 28 projects that could come online over the next five years and the potential to produce seven million tonnes or nine billion litres of SAF by 2030.
Countries such as India, China, Indonesia and Malaysia have emerged as key feedstock hubs for future SAF production, he said.
However, he said governments should prioritise increasing supply before introducing mandates.
"Our message to governments across Asia Pacific is to focus on increasing SAF supply."
He added that CORSIA remains the aviation industry's preferred global market-based measure to reduce emissions with Vietnam becoming the latest country in the region to participate in the scheme this year.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Recognition for Orang Asli in SAR reflects value of indigenous knowledge
Article:
TAPAH: The appreciation and assistance extended by Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi to the Orang Asli families involved in the search and rescue (SAR) operation for missing climber Jaslinda Saludin has been described as meaningful recognition of the community's contributions and capabilities in supporting emergency response efforts.
Special Officer to the Perak Menteri Besar for Orang Asli Community Affairs Chew Ho Beng said the gesture highlighted the importance of the Orang Asli community's deep knowledge of forests and remote terrain, particularly in operations involving challenging environments.
He said the active participation of Orang Asli volunteers throughout the operation demonstrated a strong spirit of cooperation and a willingness to assist authorities by navigating dense forest areas to help expedite the search for the victim.
"The appreciation expressed by the deputy prime minister is very meaningful because it recognises the Orang Asli community as a strategic partner to rescue teams in operations conducted in forest areas.
"It also proves that their traditional knowledge and experience of the forest environment are valuable assets that can be utilised in emergency and rescue operations," he told Bernama when met at Kampung Orang Asli Ampang Woh here today.
Chew also thanked the government for its concern for the families of Orang Asli volunteers who took part in the operation by providing assistance to them through the Department of Orang Asli Development (Jakoa).
In addition, he expressed gratitude to the Perak Menteri Besar's Office, the Tapah member of parliament and the office of the MCA deputy president for their contributions towards safeguarding the welfare of the volunteers and their families.
Yesterday, Zahid, in a Facebook post, expressed his gratitude following the successful discovery of Jaslinda, who had been reported missing while hiking at Gunung Batu Putih in Tapah.
He also thanked all parties involved in the SAR operation, including security personnel, rescue agencies, volunteers and members of the Orang Asli community whose efforts contributed to locating the victim.
As a gesture of appreciation for their commitment and sacrifices, Zahid said Jakoa had channelled assistance to the families of the Orang Asli volunteers who participated in the operation.
– Bernama
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: South Korea vows to take stern action against speculative won trading
Article:
SEOUL: South Korean Finance Minister Koo Yun-cheol on Sunday vowed stern action against speculative and illegal currency trading, as the authorities scramble to curb the won's sharp decline.
He was speaking during an emergency meeting to review markets, the ministry said in a statement.
He said financial authorities will not tolerate the won's extreme volatility and one-sided moves caused by speculative trading.
The Korean won breached the key psychological level of 1,550 per dollar late on Friday night, hitting its weakest since 2009.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: No one bigger than DAP, says Loke on impact of Marina's exit
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: DAP secretary-general Anthony Loke has dismissed talk that former Skudai assemblyman Marina Ibrahim's exit from the party would damage its standing among Malay voters.
"DAP is a party of 60 years. We have withstood all challenges, and no single individual is larger than the party. The party has always prevailed," he said.
Loke said party leaders and elected representatives came and went, but the party itself endured.
"The party is here to stay. Yes, there will be challenges, yes, there will be setbacks, but we will soldier on," he said on the sidelines of the launch of a documentary in conjunction with DAP's 60th anniversary.
On May 31, Marina announced that she would
retire from politics
and not defend her seat.
Johor DAP chairman Teo Nie Ching later described Marina's decision to quit politics and not defend her seat in the looming state polls as "a significant setback to the party's electoral strategy."
Subsequently, a letter purportedly from Marina went viral, in which she allegedly rejected an offer to contest the Tiram state seat and a chairmanship in a statutory body.
However, Marina
declined to confirm
the authenticity of the letter, saying the issue was giving her a "headache".
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] Six, including baby, killed in Sungai Petani crash
Article:
SUNGAI PETANI: Six people, including a toddler and an infant, were killed, while a 3-year-old girl was seriously injured when their sports utility vehicle collided with a 10-tonne tipper truck at Kampung Baru Pinang Tunggal today.
The deceased were identified as Proton X50 driver Ahmad Shafiq Ahmad Shukri, 27, Nora Mhd Husin, 55, Jamaliah Sannusi, 27, Ahmad Fahim Ahmad Shukri, 21, a 7-year-old boy and a 6-month-old baby.
Aulia Sofea Ahmad Shafiq, 3, suffered serious injuries in the 3.46pm crash.
Tikam Batu Fire and Rescue Station chief Fauzi Shuaib said personnel from the Tikam Batu and Sungai Petani stations were dispatched to the scene after receiving an emergency call.
He said there were seven occupants in the SUV and six were confirmed dead at the scene.
The injured girl was taken to hospital, while the truck driver escaped unhurt.
Fauzi said the victims trapped in the SUV had to be extricated and that debris from the crash was still being cleared.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 31 jellyfish sting cases recorded at Besut beach since May
Article:
JERTIH: The Civil Defence Force in Besut has recorded 31 venomous jellyfish sting cases involving victims aged between three and 44 at Pantai Bukit Keluang from May until Thursday.
Besut Civil Defence officer Captain Mohd Fazlien Jaafar said all the victims were visitors who had been swimming at the popular beach.
"In May alone, 23 individuals were reported to have been stung by venomous jellyfish, while another eight cases were recorded up to last Friday this month," he said.
"These figures are based on victims who sought initial treatment at the APM observation tower at Bukit Keluang Beach after being stung by the marine creature," he said,
He said the authorities could not rule out the possibility that more beachgoers had been stung while swimming at the location but had not reported the incidents.
Meanwhile, Fazlien said victims who sought assistance at the APM observation tower were given immediate treatment by personnel on duty.
He said those stung by jellyfish typically experienced skin redness and pain in the affected parts of the body, particularly the hands.
"APM personnel will spray the affected area with a special solution before advising victims who experience allergic reactions to seek further treatment at a hospital immediately.
"The large number of visitors to the beach during the holiday season last month contributed to the relatively high number of sting cases recorded," he said.
He said the presence of jellyfish in the area was first detected last month and their numbers were expected to decline gradually.
He advised visitors planning to swim or engage in water activities at the beach to remain vigilant and exercise caution to avoid jellyfish stings.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Health Ministry targets RM1.8bil health infrastructure boost for Melaka
Article:
MELAKA: The Health Ministry plans to develop several health infrastructure projects in Melaka worth RM1.8 billion under the Rolling Plan 2 of the 13th Malaysia Plan (13MP).
Minister Datuk Seri Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad said the planned infrastructure developments included a Type 2 specialist hospital covering 24.2ha in Ayer Keroh here, costing an estimated RM875 million.
"The hospital will have 400 beds and focus on providing cardiology, cardiothoracic, neurosurgery and ambulatory care centre services.
"In addition, we are expediting the construction of the RM697 million 476-bed capacity Women and Children's Block at Melaka Hospital, which is currently in the tender process, to resolve capacity issues at the hospital," he said after a working visit to Melaka Hospital today.
Also present were Hang Tuah Jaya member of parliament Adam Adli Abd Halim, who is also the deputy higher education minister, state Health, Human Resources and Unity Committee chairman Datuk Ngwe Hee Sem and Melaka Health Department director Dr Husnina Ibrahim.
Dzulkefly said the ministry also planned to demolish and rebuild the 64-year-old Jasin Health Clinic (Type 2) and Jasin District Integrated Health Complex at an estimated cost of RM126.99 million.
He said the project would include the construction of a new Jasin District Health Office.
He added that the ministry would seek approval for preliminary work details under Rolling Plan 2 of the 13MP for the Bukit Katil Health Clinic (Type 2).
The clinic is expected to cost RM50 million and become a fully equipped health referral centre to accommodate a population density of over 80,243 by 2030.
"The ministry will also seek preliminary work details under Rolling Plan 2 of the 13MP for the Duyong Health Clinic (Type 2), costing an estimated RM50 million. This is aimed at supporting operations at the Ujong Pasir Health Clinic, which is facing severe space constraints.
"These efforts reflect the Unity Government's full commitment to ensuring the health ecosystem in Melaka remains competitive," he said.
Meanwhile, Dzulkefly said he had approved a special allocation of RM6 million to resolve facility repairs and upgrades, as well as the urgent need for medical and non-medical assets in the six facilities he visited.
The six facilities are the Durian Tunggal Health Clinic, Jasin Health Clinic, Jasin Hospital, Bukit Katil Rural Clinic, Melaka Hospital and Alor Gajah Hospital.
"I hope that this immediate allocation approval and the long-term planning will provide comfort to patients and facilitate the work of health personnel," he said.
During his working visit, Dzulkefly also launched a mobile dental clinic at the Jasin Health Clinic to ensure wider access to dental health for the local community.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Goku, Luffy bring anime magic to NSTP cosplay contest at KL Book Fair
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: New Straits Times Press brought the virtual world to life this weekend by hosting an online cosplay competition in conjunction with the Kuala Lumpur International Book Fair.
Participants from across the country brought action heroes to life, performing signature stunts that thrilled a crowd of excited children.
The champion was Azwan Adizar, 39, from Rawang, who won first prize with his portrayal of Son Goku.
Azwan, who won RM300, said he discovered the competition through NSTP's Facebook page and decided to participate because of his passion for cosplay.
"My wife and sister helped prepare the costume, while my wife also did my make-up. I don't do this for money. I enjoy seeing children happy when they meet their favourite characters," he said.
Azwan, who began cosplaying three years ago and owns five costumes, said he also introduced his son, Aryan Azfar, to the hobby to help build his confidence.
Aryan, 11, who dressed up as Monkey D. Luffy, finished third and received RM100.
Second-place winner Muhammad Rozaidi Abdullah, 23, took home RM200 for his Spider-Man portrayal.
Rozaidi said his interest in cosplay began at the age of 16 while working as a mascot promoter at a night market in his hometown in Tanjung Malim, Perak.
"Someone later invited me to appear at a birthday party in costume, and that's when I became interested in cosplay. The prizes are secondary; it's the passion that keeps us involved," he said.
Meanwhile, NSTP manager of business development and special projects Tengku Kaifimirza Tuan Cherit @ Tengku Hamzah said the competition received an encouraging response after it was promoted through the company's social media platforms for the first time a few days ago.
"The competition was mostly online, where participants were required to dress up as a cosplay character, photograph themselves and send in their entries to the designated address.
"Participants were also required to tag their cosplay photos on NST Online Instagram and their own handles to encourage more traffic to the publication's official website and stories.
"The judges would then evaluate and choose the top three best costumes based on accuracy, creativity and several other criteria.
"The response today, especially with the cosplay winners in their character suits, grabbed the attention of fair visitors, especially the younger generation and children.
"We wanted to tap into the growing popularity of cosplay and engage younger audiences through a platform they are familiar with. Although this cosplay competition is the first of its kind organised by NSTP, I believe this might be the start of many more similar competitions to come," he said when met at the Kuala Lumpur International Book Fair at World Trade Centre Kuala Lumpur today.
Tengku Kaifimirza said the prize-giving ceremony held at the TV3 Tonton booth became an instant attraction with the presence of mascots such as Agent Ali, Sun Goku, Spider-Man and Monkey D. Luffy.
Visitors, especially children, were excited to interact with the cosplay winners and take photographs with them.
"Based on the response we received, we see strong potential to organise similar events in the future," he said.
This year's Kuala Lumpur International Book Fair at the World Trade Centre Kuala Lumpur featured more than 1,200 booths and drew thousands of visitors throughout its 10-day run.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Missing US tourist on Pulau Tioman rescued after sharing real-time location
Article:
KUANTAN: An American tourist reported missing while hiking on Pulau Tioman was found safe last night after sharing his real-time location via mobile phone with rescue teams.
Rompin district police chief Superintendent Sharif Shai Sharif Mondoi said police received information about the missing man at 5.30pm yesterday after he failed to return to his resort following a hike along the old Kampung Tekek-Kampung Juara trail.
"The victim was found at 8.33pm in the Bukit Parang forest area, Kampung Tekek, safe and without injuries. He was taken out of the forest area for further checks," Sharif Shai said in a statement today.
He said the trail has many junctions and side paths and is no longer used as a main route by locals.
"At present, the trail is often used by tourists as a hiking and recreational route.
"Investigations found that the victim got lost after taking the wrong branch of the path, which caused him to be unable to exit the hilly forest area," he said.
Sharif Shai said the search operation was carried out by the Tioman police station with assistance from the Wildlife and National Parks Department team on Pulau Tioman.
He advised tourists engaging in hiking or recreational activities in forested areas to make adequate preparations, inform relevant parties of their movements, and ensure communication devices are in good working order to avoid untoward incidents.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Border security drive sees 91 illegal jetties demolished
Article:
KOTA BARU: A total of 91 illegal jetties built on government land along the Malaysia-Thailand border have been demolished as part of ongoing efforts to curb cross-border crime and smuggling activities.
National Security Council director-general Datuk Raja Nushirwan Zainal Abidin said the structures were erected illegally on government-owned land and their removal was necessary to strengthen border security.
"All 91 illegal jetties that we have demolished were built on government land," he told reporters after attending a border security-related meeting here today.
Raja Nushirwan said border authorities had so far carried out the demolition works using existing allocations and there were currently no plans to seek additional funding.
"At the moment, we are still using the existing allocation. No decision has been made on additional allocations.
"We will maintain the current funding for now," he said.
Raja Nushirwan said the government recognised the need to ensure residents affected by the closure of illegal border access points were provided with alternative economic opportunities.
"With the assistance of the police and the Malaysian Armed Forces, we have demolished the illegal jetties.
"To be fair to the local residents, we must also help develop economic activities in the area.
"We are working closely with the state government and the Implementation Coordination Unit (ICU) at the federal level to coordinate these efforts," he said.
The demolition of illegal jetties since last year was part of broader measures by security agencies to tighten control along the Malaysia-Thailand border and prevent smuggling, illegal entry and other transnational crimes.
Border authorities have identified 213 illegal jetties along the border, built on both government and private land.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] Unity above all, reject divisive racial politics, says PM
Article:
SUNGAI PETANI: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has urged Malaysians to reject divisive racial narratives and strengthen unity for national stability and progress.
He warned of external and internal agendas portraying communities as threatened by one another, saying such rhetoric risks eroding trust in a multiracial nation.
"Malaysia's strength lies in cooperation among the Malays, Chinese, Indians and other communities. Our economic progress is not built on division but on shared purpose.
"Political stability has underpinned Malaysia's economic resilience, including currency strength and investor confidence, which depend on continued national unity," Anwar said.
He said this at the launch of the Himpunan Rakan Muda Madani at Padang Awam Taman Keladi here this afternoon.
Recalling the 1969 racial unrest and pessimism expressed by foreign media at the time, Anwar said Malaysia had proven critics wrong by rebuilding through reconciliation and inclusive growth.
Anwar said the focus must now shift to education, skills development and job creation, particularly for young Malaysians entering new economic sectors requiring training and adaptability.
He also stressed the importance of youth empowerment programmes, including structured initiatives under the Madani framework, to cultivate values, discipline and leadership among the younger generation.
Anwar said development must go beyond infrastructure and digital progress, including AI, to also strengthen moral and social values rooted in responsibility, family and community.
He emphasised that constitutional guarantees on Islam, Malay rights, Bahasa Melayu and the monarchy remain protected, and should not be used as tools to divide society.
"At the same time, all communities must be treated fairly, including access to education systems, schools and economic opportunities without ethnic barriers.
"Rural Malays, urban Chinese entrepreneurs and Indian professionals are not in competition, but collectively contribute to national growth," he added.
He cautioned against "old politics" that incite fear, urging Malaysians instead to embrace cooperation, mutual respect and human dignity as core national values.
Malaysia, he said, must be safeguarded as a successful model of a diverse nation, with unity and youth participation as key drivers of future progress.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] Bersatu vows to preserve unity with Pas in PN
Article:
New Straits Times
KUALA LUMPUR: Bersatu has reiterated its commitment to maintaining good relations with Pas within the Perikatan Nasional (PN) coalition, amid speculation over tensions between the two parties.
Bersatu president Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin said he had received a letter from the Pas secretary-general yesterday regarding the relationship between both parties, as well as proposals to strengthen PN.
"I will give due consideration to the proposals and bring the matter to the Bersatu supreme council meeting for further deliberation.
"Bersatu reaffirms its commitment and determination to maintain good relations with Pas within Perikatan Nasional," he said in a statement today.
The development comes amid renewed tensions between the two parties, after Pas president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang reportedly said the party would re-evaluate its cooperation with Bersatu following several actions seen as going against the spirit of unity within PN.
https://www.nst.com.my/news/nation/2026/05/1445709/pas-re-evaluate-cooperation-bersatu-says-hadi
The Marang member of parliament was reported to have described the actions as "unpleasant", prompting further discussions within the coalition.
In response, Bersatu information chief Datuk Tun Faisal Ismail Aziz said the party would raise matters relating to its cooperation with Pas for detailed discussion at upcoming political bureau and supreme council meetings.
Pas subsequently held an
emergency meeting on May 24
to discuss issues concerning PN, including its future relationship with Bersatu.
The meeting, chaired by Hadi in Kuala Terengganu, involved senior leadership, including members of the Pas political bureau and central committee.
Hadi said Pas would make a decision on its
political cooperation
with Bersatu tomorrow.
He said the matter would be discussed at the party's central committee meeting following deliberations by the party's Syura Council.
Hadi added that the syura council had decided to leave the final decision to the central committee, which would announce the party's next course of action.
On June 2, the
Pas syura council
was reported to have discussed the party's cooperation with Bersatu, with Central Committee member Tan Sri Annuar Musa saying the party was "paying close attention" to current political developments and their impact on national politics.
He added that all aspects, including Pas' relationship with Bersatu, were taken into consideration.

Title: Explainer: What to do when a parking tout tries to charge you a fee
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Many Malaysians have encountered parking touts demanding payment for parking in public areas, often leaving motorists uncertain about whether such fees are legitimate.
Parking touts are those who illegally or unofficially collect parking fees from drivers despite having no legal authority to do so.
Although enforcement agencies have conducted numerous operations against these individuals, questions persist over whether parking touts have any right to collect fees in public spaces.
Authorities have repeatedly made it clear that they do not.
On May 16, during an enforcement operation at Jalan Yew and Jalan Pasar Baharu in Pudu, Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) detained three men for illegally collecting parking fees.
City Hall said the
detainees were fined
between RM1,200 and RM2,500 and jailed for between two and three months following their conviction under Section 50(3) of the Road Transport Act 1987.
⁠According to criminal lawyer Rajsurian Pillai, Section 50(3) of the Road Transport Act 1987 states that, "any person, without lawful authority to be on a road or at a parking place for the purpose of directing motorists where to park or soliciting money in relation to the parking or supervision of vehicles commits an offence.
"Those found guilty under the Act may be liable to a fine of up to RM2,000 or imprisonment for up to six months, or both.
"If caught in such a situation, call the police or local council," Pillai told the New Straits Times.
On April 9, City Hall detained three foreigners during an enforcement operation against parking touts. The detainees were later fined RM1,500 each following a court order.
Separately, social media users have shared experiences of being approached by parking touts, with some saying they felt pressured to pay out of fear that their vehicles could be vandalised if they refused.
This came after a viral
clip surfaced on X,
garnering more than 249,000 views. Posted by user @kamaghul, it praised a man who confronted a tout who attempted to collect a parking fee from him.
"You can't do this here," he said, pointing to the group of people attempting to collect parking fees from him.
Several users responded by recalling their experiences of being approached by parking touts.
Recently, user @Bigsnorlax1985 dubbed the parking touts "ulat" who illegally collect parking fees from drivers parking their vehicles by roadsides or in public spaces.
"Back in the day, people used to experience these kinds of "ulat" collecting parking fees like this. If you didn't pay up, they'd scratch your car. Usually addicts or junkies. But when Malaysia got a bit more advanced, the cops started catching them," the user said.
Another user, @erufanali said, "sometimes I would call them out but I'm scared that when I come back there might be someone who breaks into the car so just give it a go."
Rajsurian also noted that those threatened by a parking tout may take legal action under Section 506 of the Penal Code.
The code states that, "whoever commits the offence of criminal intimidation shall be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to two years or with fine or with both."
In the case of death threats or attempts to cause physical hurt, Section 323 of the Penal Code states that, "voluntarily causing hurt is a criminal offence. If convicted, the offender faces a penalty of up to one year in prison, a fine of up to RM2,000, or both."
Similarly, those threatened with vandalism of personal vehicles could
take legal action under Section 426
of the Penal Code, which states that, "whoever commits mischief shall be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to five years or with fine or with both."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Endangered hawksbill turtle rescued from drain near Melaka motel
Article:
MELAKA: A hawksbill turtle trapped in a drain near a motel in Tanjung Kling was rescued by the Fire and Rescue Department in an operation today.
In the 11.30am incident, the marine reptile was spotted by a motel worker moving inside a deep drain near the premises before attempts were made to pull it out.
Motel owner Alex Tan said staff first noticed the turtle struggling in the drain and tried to extricate it, but were unable to do so due to the depth of the structure and the size of the animal.
"At first, my workers saw the turtle in the drain near the motel. We tried to get it out but were unsuccessful," he said when contacted.
Concerned for the animal's safety, Tan said he then contacted the fire department for assistance.
Tan added that turtles straying into the area were not uncommon, as the nearby beach is a known nesting site.
He said similar sightings had occurred previously, including a case where a dead turtle was found in the vicinity.
"Turtles wandering into the area have happened a few times before, and we have even found a turtle carcass. However, this is the first time one has been found trapped in a drain," he said.
[[nid:1457444]]
A Melaka Fire and Rescue Department spokesman said seven firemen from the Tangga Batu Fire and Rescue Station were deployed to the scene after receiving an emergency call.
The team, led by Senior Fire Officer I Sany Sahari, arrived within minutes and found the turtle trapped in the drain near the motel.
"Rescue work was carried out and the turtle was successfully removed safely before being released back into the sea," he said.
He added that the operation was brought under control at 11.55am and concluded shortly after, with no injuries reported.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar rejects claims he is delaying elections to stay in office
Article:
SUNGAI PETANI: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today stressed that he is not delaying the dissolution of Parliament merely to retain his position.
Instead, he said he wants to fully focus on restoring the country's economy and resolving issues affecting the people.
He added that political instability and frequent elections would only hurt investor confidence and disrupt government efforts to attract quality investments.
"I want to focus on solving the people's problems first. Politics must be stable. I do not want politics to be used only for propaganda or to maintain positions.
"I am not postponing the election because I want to remain in power, but because I want to ensure the country's economy continues to grow and the people benefit," Anwar said.
He said this during a meeting with Kedah federal department heads and the federal Village Development and Security Committees (JPKKP) at Universiti Teknologi Mara (UiTM) Merbok here.
Anwar said the government is currently focusing on strengthening the economy, attracting high-tech investments, and raising the people's standard of living while addressing grassroots needs.
He said the recent strengthening of the ringgit proves that the country's economic position is becoming more stable due to structured and effective economic policies.
"Economic management is crucial, and politics must be stable. If elections are held every month and the political situation is unstable, who would want to invest?
"Look at neighbouring countries and the strength of their currencies. Today, many travel to Indonesia and other countries and can see that the ringgit is stronger than before," he said.
He added that Malaysia can handle various challenges, including stabilising the currency, stimulating economic growth, and attracting foreign investment, provided that political stability is maintained.
Anwar also stressed that the federal government maintains good relations with all state governments, including those under the opposition, as the development of the people remains the top priority.
"Even though I do not have absolute power in the country's administration, I want to use the trust given to me to help the people and strengthen the economy.
"Allah has tested me with various trials, including going in and out of prison. That is why I do not want to use power to enrich my children, wife, or family," he said.
He reminded leaders and the public to remain patient and steadfast in supporting the national development agenda implemented by the Madani government.
He said nation-building is not an easy process as it requires patience, wisdom, and continuous commitment despite facing various criticisms.
"There will always be various criticisms, but the mandate given by the people must continue to be carried out with full responsibility for the country's interest," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MACC uncovers RM9mil false claims in Daya Kerjaya programme
Article:
KUALA TERENGGANU: The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has uncovered irregularities involving the government's Daya Kerjaya 2.0 programme, with false claims amounting to RM9.01 million.
MACC Chief Commissioner Datuk Seri Abd Halim Aman said investigations showed that 143 companies involving 320 workers nationwide were linked to the alleged misuse of government funds under the Social Security Organisation (Perkeso) initiative in 2024 and 2025.
An operation is expected to begin next week.
Under the Daya Kerjaya 2.0 programme, the government provides employers with a wage incentive of RM1,500 a month for six months for each eligible worker hired, including persons with disabilities, former prisoners and senior citizens.
"However, based on our preliminary intelligence gathering, several issues have been identified in the implementation of the programme.
"Among them, we found individuals who were declared as employees but did not actually exist," he said during a visit to the Terengganu MACC office here today.
Abd Halim said investigators also detected cases involving workers who did not meet the minimum six-month employment requirement but received the full incentive payment, as well as the use of forged documents to obtain the payments.
He said the MACC had identified another 1,638 companies suspected of engaging in similar activities involving about RM45 million, although those cases remain at a preliminary stage of investigation.
Abd Halim said the commission has received good cooperation from Perkeso headquarters throughout the investigation and will continue working closely with the agency's state offices to facilitate operations, including the collection of information and evidence.
He said the MACC views seriously any elements of abuse or weaknesses in the management of government funds intended to benefit the public.
Abd Halim said the commission's Governance Investigation division would intensify efforts to assist government departments, agencies and companies in improving governance and strengthening procedures to prevent leakages and misuse of public funds.
"If there are weaknesses in the system or procedures, we will help identify them and recommend improvements to ensure government initiatives are implemented more effectively and reach the intended beneficiaries," he said.
"The Daya Kerjaya 2.0 case is one example of how a government incentive introduced with good intentions to help the people and create employment opportunities can be abused by irresponsible parties," he said.
Separately, Abd Halim said the MACC plans to establish Anti-Corruption Cadets at secondary schools to promote awareness and instil a culture of integrity and anti-corruption values among the younger generation.
As a start, he said the commission is considering launching the programme at one or two schools in Putrajaya or Kuala Lumpur involving between 30 and 40 students.
"Of course, we will first discuss the proposal with the Education Ministry. We will then look at whether the ministry agrees to it, as well as any constraints and the costs involved, such as uniforms and other requirements," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PM rules out fuel price hike, says subsidies shield rakyat
Article:
SUNGAI PETANI: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has ruled out any increase in fuel prices, saying the government is already absorbing billions of ringgit monthly to protect Malaysians from global oil shocks.
He said Malaysia continues to maintain one of the lowest retail fuel prices globally at RM1.99 per litre, despite volatile international oil markets driven by geopolitical tensions.
He said the government spends an estimated RM3 billion to RM5 billion every month on fuel-related subsidies, with costs spiking further during periods of global price surges.
"At certain points, the monthly subsidy bill reached about RM5 billion. Now, it is about RM3 billion to RM4 billion due to price fluctuations.
"Raising fuel prices to match market levels is not an option, as it will directly burden households, transport operators, farmers, fishermen and small businesses.
"I will not agree to that (raising fuel prices) as suggested by some economists," he said at the launch of Himpunan Rakan Muda Madani at Padang Awam Taman Keladi here this afternoon.
Anwar said some of Malaysia's neighbours are now paying RM10.10, RM4 and RM3.80 for fuel respectively.
"Yet, we maintain ours at RM1.99.
"We also have targeted assistance such as the Budi Madani subsidy programme for eligible diesel users, including fishermen, farmers and school bus operators, to help ease their burdens. This will remain a key shield for the rakyat," he added.
He said billions are being spent every month to stabilise prices, warning that passing costs on to the people would increase prices across the board.
"We are able to do all this because of cost-cutting measures," he said, adding that spending must be carefully managed amid concerns over wastage and leakages.
He warned that sustaining higher subsidies indefinitely would strain public finances, but said the government would continue prioritising efficiency, anti-corruption measures and better-targeted aid rather than withdrawing support abruptly.
Anwar also cautioned against policy suggestions that could trigger inflation, saying any fuel price hike would have a cascading effect on goods and services.
He added that savings from improved governance would be redirected towards community development, including youth programmes, infrastructure and public facilities.
At the end of his speech, Anwar approved a RM2 million allocation for the construction of a community hall in Taman Keladi, which he said could be used by young people for various activities.
The allocation followed a request from Youth and Sports Minister Dr Mohammed Taufiq Johari, who is also Sungai Petani member of parliament.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Angler drowns in Serendah lake
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A 50-year-old man drowned while fishing at a lake behind the Serendah Health Clinic today.
Selangor Fire and Rescue Department Zone 6 chief Mustakim Rimon said the department received a distress call at 12.06pm.
He said four firemen from the Kuala Kubu Baru Fire and Rescue station were dispatched to the scene, about 28km away.
"The victim is believed to have drowned while fishing at the lake.
"The body was recovered by firemen with assistance from the Batang Kali Volunteer Fire Brigade," he said in a statement today.
Mustakim said Health Ministry personnel pronounced the victim dead at the scene before the body was handed over to police.
The operation concluded at 2.16pm.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Agrobank brings financing to market for hawkers, micro traders
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Agrobank has expanded its outreach to hawkers and micro-entrepreneurs by bringing its financing solutions directly to the Kelana Jaya farmers' market to improve access to funding for small businesses.
The engagement session, attended by Finance Minister II Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah Azizan, forms part of the ministry's initiative aimed at strengthening engagement with small traders and better understanding the challenges faced by the micro-enterprise community.
Agrobank's participation was also in line with Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim's call for financial institutions to engage directly with small businesses and help them secure the capital needed to start, sustain and expand their operations.
During the session, traders shared concerns ranging from rising operating costs and rental expenses to market access challenges.
The feedback is expected to help Agrobank further enhance its financial and non-financial support programmes for hawkers and micro-entrepreneurs.
Agrobank president and group chief executive officer Tengku Datuk Ahmad Badli Shah Raja Hussin said farmers' markets play an important role in supporting local economic activity and entrepreneurship.
"Farmers' markets are not only platforms for small traders to sell their products, but also form part of the local economic ecosystem.
"By engaging directly with traders, we can ensure they receive clear information on the financing facilities available and help them make informed decisions to grow their businesses," he said in a statement.
Tengku Ahmad Badli said Agrobank remained committed to bringing its services closer to customers rather than relying solely on branch visits.
Agrobank also set up an information booth at the market to provide details on Agro Penjaja-i and other financing solutions tailored to the needs of small traders.
The bank currently serves more than 15,000 customers within the hawker segment and plans to further strengthen its outreach to more hawkers and micro-entrepreneurs nationwide.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Rescued hiker Jaslinda in stable condition, recovering
Article:
TAPAH: The condition of hiker Jaslinda Saludin, 49, who is receiving treatment at Tapah Hospital, is reported to be improving.
Tapah Hospital director Dr Wan Immi Salim said she had sustained several injuries after spending an extended period in the forest.
However, she said Jaslinda remains in stable condition, although she still requires further medical treatment and close monitoring.
"Jaslinda is currently stable. However, she sustained several injuries after being in the forest for a prolonged period.
"She received the necessary treatment after arriving here at 9.25pm last night and is still undergoing close medical care.
"She has multiple wounds on her body, and there are some injuries to her legs. Nevertheless, her condition remains stable," she told Buletin TV3 here today.
Dr Wan Immi said only immediate family members are currently allowed to visit Jaslinda.
"Her family is here and she is being accompanied by her sister throughout her stay.
"The duration of her treatment will depend on her day-to-day recovery and progress.
"Today, she appears more energetic and cheerful, and she is happy to see so many people asking about her wellbeing after spending two weeks without speaking to anyone.
"At this time, visits are restricted to family members and next of kin to respect her privacy and allow for a better recovery process," she said.
Yesterday, Jaslinda was admitted to Tapah Hospital for further treatment after being
found safe
at the Orang Asli settlement of Kampung Lubuk Gaharu in Pos Musoh.
Her discovery brought an end to a 14-day search and rescue (SAR) operation involving multiple agencies after she was reported missing during the Trans Spencer Chapman hiking expedition at Gunung Batu Putih.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Police target influencers, online syndicates in World Cup gambling crackdown
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Police have warned social media influencers against promoting illegal gambling activities as authorities brace for an anticipated surge in online betting linked to the upcoming FIFA World Cup.
Bukit Aman Criminal Investigation Department (CID) director Datuk M. Kumar said police intelligence indicated that almost 90 per cent of illegal betting activities during the tournament would take place online, with syndicates increasingly relying on digital platforms, messaging applications and social media to recruit customers.
"We are particularly concerned about the growing trend of influencers being used to advertise illegal gambling platforms to their followers.
"Police are aware of the trend of social media influencers being used to promote illegal gambling activities on social media platforms," he told the New Straits Times.
He said action would be taken against those involved, as promoting gambling is an offence under the Common Gaming Houses Act 1953.
The 2026 FIFA World Cup, which runs from June 11 to July 19 and features 48 participating nations, is expected to attract massive global viewership and heightened betting activity.
Kumar said the football fever surrounding the tournament was likely to be exploited by local and international gambling syndicates seeking to profit through illegal wagering platforms.
"Unlike previous tournaments, where betting activities were largely centred on traditional bookmakers, current projections suggest that online gambling will dominate this year's World Cup betting landscape.
"Syndicates now prefer operating through closed groups on messaging applications and social media platforms to reduce the risk of detection by authorities," he said, adding that there was also an increasing tendency to use cashless transactions and electronic payment methods to facilitate betting activities.
According to Kumar, gambling operators typically lure customers with promises of lucrative returns, registration bonuses, free credits and live betting opportunities during matches.
Some syndicates, he added, also employ agents and intermediaries to recruit punters and direct them to betting platforms under their control.
To counter the threat, Kumar said police would intensify intelligence gathering, monitoring and enforcement efforts against individuals and syndicates involved in organising, promoting or participating in illegal gambling activities.
He said Op Soga would be launched nationwide throughout the tournament, involving enforcement personnel at contingent and district levels to detect, raid and arrest bookmakers as well as punters.
Police are also working closely with the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) to carry out real-time blocking of websites offering illegal betting services.
Kumar said offenders could face action under the Betting Act 1953, the Common Gaming Houses Act 1953 and, where applicable, the Anti-Money Laundering, Anti-Terrorism Financing and Proceeds of Unlawful Activities Act 2001.
He urged the public not to be enticed by betting schemes promising easy profits, warning that such activities often lead to financial losses, gambling addiction and other social problems.
Members of the public with information on illegal gambling activities are encouraged to report the matter to the police.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: No MyKad visible in viral video of alleged Myanmar national, say police
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Police have found that no identity document or MyKad was shown in a viral video alleging that a Myanmar national possessed a Malaysian identity card.
Putrajaya police chief Assistant Commissioner Aidi Sham Mohamed said investigations revealed that the video circulating on social media did not contain any visual evidence to support claims that the individual featured in the recording was holding a MyKad.
"Further checks found that no identification document or MyKad was displayed in the video to support the allegation being made," he said in a statement today.
He said police received a report at about 8.45am on June 5 regarding the dissemination of false information linked to the issuance of identity cards to an individual alleged to be a Myanmar citizen.
Initial investigations found that the video had been shared on Facebook through the account "Na'am Ahmad", questioning how an individual purportedly from Myanmar could possess a blue MyKad.
"Police also established that the clip was a reposted recording shared by a secondary party.
"Following the findings, the matter has been referred to the National Registration Department (NRD) for further verification and investigations to determine the authenticity of the claims," he said.
It was reported that the NRD confirmed it had lodged a police report over the viral video and said citizenship status could not be verified solely based on social media content.
The department said the identity of the individual in the video had yet to be established and that it had sought the assistance of the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) to trace the original source of the recording and identify those involved in its dissemination.
Aidi Sham urged the public not to spread unverified or false information related to the incident, warning that it could create confusion and tarnish the reputation of those involved.
He also advised the public to obtain information from official sources and channel any relevant information to the police to assist investigations.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 'Our prayers have been answered': Jaslinda's daughter on mum's rescue
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: One of Jaslinda Saludin's daughters has described her mother's rescue as an answered prayer, thanking Malaysians and search personnel who never gave up hope during the search for the missing hiker.
In a post on Threads, Dhia Damia Mohd Faizal said she was grateful that her mother was found yesterday, two weeks after she went missing during a hike on Gunung Batu Putih.
She said she, her father and her elder sister had gone to the foot of the mountain on the morning of the discovery to pray and recite the Yasin.
"Alhamdulillah. Praise be to Allah. There is nothing impossible with His power.
"Only Allah knows how we felt at the time. We surrendered ourselves to Allah. That evening, by His will, Allah answered all our prayers," she wrote.
Dhia also thanked Malaysians who had continuously prayed for her mother's safe return throughout the ordeal.
She also conveyed the family's appreciation to the Orang Asli community, search and rescue personnel, police, firefighters, the General Operations Force, mountain guides, non-governmental organisations, district officials and all others involved in the search operation.
"Only Allah can repay your kindness."
Dhia added that Jaslinda would remain in the hospital for several days for
treatment and recovery
.
"For now, please give her some space to rest. My mother is also deeply grateful to everyone."
Jaslinda was
found
by Orang Asli villagers near Kampung Lubuk Gaharu in Pos Musoh after a 14-day search and rescue operation.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Former Kedah deputy speaker shared final dinner after daughter's engagement
Article:
ALOR STAR: Nur Anisa Mohd Jamil's last memory of her father was sharing dinner after her engagement ceremony, a day before he was found drowned today.
The daughter of former Kedah deputy speaker Datuk Mohd Jamil Md Idross described him as a devoted father who spent much of his time at home and at the mosque after retiring from politics.
Nur Anisa, 27, the youngest child of Mohd Jamil, said her father appeared healthy and in good spirits during her engagement ceremony yesterday.
"He was able to see me get engaged at a ceremony held yesterday, and he looked healthy.
"Last night, my sister, my fiancé and I had bihun sup with my father at home. He ate with a very good appetite. That was the last meal we shared," she said.
Mohd Jamil, 78, a former Pengkalan Kundor assemblyman, was found drowned in Sungai Teluk Bagan in Tandop today.
Nur Anisa said her father had largely withdrawn from public life after leaving politics, preferring to spend his time gardening, attending the mosque and being with his family.
"After dinner last night, I told him that I had locked all the doors before going to bed, and he said it was okay.
"But at about 5am today, my mother, sister, and I realised he was not in the house.
"We looked for him at the mosque because we thought he might have gone there for dawn prayers, but he wasn't there.
"We continued searching and eventually saw his Perodua Kancil parked by the river, along with his slippers nearby," she said when met at the Forensic Department of Sultanah Bahiyah Hospital.
She said the family was shocked by the tragedy and never imagined they would lose their loved one so suddenly.
Nur Anisa said the family frequently took Mohd Jamil on holidays, with their most recent trip being to Kuala Lumpur on May 30 to celebrate her mother's birthday.
"He won't be able to see me get married next January, but I'm grateful that he was there to witness my engagement yesterday," she said.
Meanwhile, his wife, Datin Noor Hasita Mat Isa, 58, who is Kedah Wanita Umno secretary, said her husband had dedicated more than four decades to Umno and public service.
She said he served two terms as Pengkalan Kundor assemblyman from 1995 to 1999 and from 2004 to 2008, before becoming Kedah deputy speaker from 2018 to 2023.
"He looked healthy and showed no signs of illness. We never expected that he would leave us so suddenly," she said.
Noor Hasita said the funeral prayers and burial would be held at the Teluk Bagan Mosque Muslim Cemetery in Tandop before Asar prayers today.
Earlier, authorities confirmed that
Mohd Jamil was found drowned in Sungai Teluk Bagan
at about 8.10am.
His body was recovered by personnel from the Jalan Raja Fire and Rescue Station and later handed over to the police for further action.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Jaslinda found note left by mountain guide during search for her
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Hiker Jaslinda Saludin, 49, found a note left behind by rescuers at Kem Air Busuk on Gunung Batu Putih while search and rescue (SAR) operations were underway.
Perak Fire and Rescue Department Operations Division assistant director Sabarodzi Nor Ahmad said Jaslinda brought the note out with her and showed it to firefighters yesterday after she was rescued.
"The note was left by mountain guide Hafis at Kem Air Busuk while he was conducting a search in the area.
"At the time, search efforts were focused on the route between Pos Musoh and Kem Air Busuk on May 30.
"Jaslinda only reached the area yesterday," he said.
Jaslinda was found
after being missing for 14 days during a hiking expedition at Gunung Batu Putih in Tapah, Perak, and is currently staying at the Tok Batin's house in Kampung Lubuk Gaharu.
The matter was confirmed by Perak Fire and Rescue Department director Datuk Sayani Saidon.
Jaslinda had joined a group of 13 others and two Forestry Department mountain guides on the Trans Spencer Chapman expedition, which began at 2am on May 23 via the Pos Gedung–Gunung Bah Gading–Gunung Batu Putih–Kuala Woh route.
She and another hiker, Mohd Hanafi Neikmad, 41, had earlier experienced health issues and stopped hiking.
However, the supply chain executive at a private company reportedly continued towards the summit and was last seen on May 24.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MACC uncovers ghost workers, forged documents in RM9m Daya Kerjaya 2.0 claims
Article:
KUALA TERENGGANU, June 7 — The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has uncovered irregularities involving the government’s Daya Kerjaya 2.0 programme, with false claims amounting to RM9.01 million.
MACC Chief Commissioner Datuk Seri Abd Halim Aman said investigations, with an operation expected to begin next week, found that 143 companies involving 320 workers nationwide were linked to the alleged misuse of government funds under the Social Security Organisation’s (Perkeso) initiative in 2024 and 2025.
“Under the Daya Kerjaya 2.0 programme, the government provides employers with a wage incentive of RM1,500 a month for six months for each eligible worker hired, including persons with disabilities, former prisoners and senior citizens.
“However, based on our preliminary intelligence gathering, several issues have been identified in the implementation of the programme. Among them, we found individuals who were declared as employees but did not actually exist,” he told Bernama during a visit to the Terengganu MACC office today.
Abd Halim said investigators also detected cases involving workers who did not meet the minimum six-month employment requirement but received the full incentive payment, as well as the use of forged documents to obtain the payments.
He said the MACC had also identified another 1,638 companies suspected of engaging in similar activities involving about RM45 million, although those cases remain at a preliminary stage of investigation.
Abd Halim said the commission has received good cooperation from Perkeso headquarters throughout the investigation and will continue working closely with the agency’s state offices to facilitate operations, including the collection of information and evidence.
He also said the MACC views seriously any elements of abuse or weaknesses in the management of government funds intended to benefit the public.
Abd Halim said the commission’s Governance Investigation Division would intensify efforts to assist government departments, agencies and companies in improving governance and strengthening procedures to prevent leakages and misuse of public funds.
“If there are weaknesses in the system or procedures, we will help identify them and recommend improvements to ensure government initiatives are implemented more effectively and reach the intended beneficiaries.
“The Daya Kerjaya 2.0 case is one example of how a government incentive introduced with good intentions to help the people and create employment opportunities can be abused by irresponsible parties,” he said.
Separately, Abd Halim said the MACC plans to establish Anti-Corruption Cadets at secondary schools to promote awareness and instil a culture of integrity and anti-corruption values among the younger generation.
As a start, he said the commission is considering launching the programme at one or two schools in Putrajaya or Kuala Lumpur involving between 30 and 40 students.
“Of course, we will first discuss the proposal with the Education Ministry. We will then look at whether the ministry agrees to it, as well as any constraints and the costs involved, such as uniforms and other requirements,” he said. — Bernama

Title: Jaslinda recovering well after 14-day ordeal in Perak jungle, hospital monitoring continues
Article:
TAPAH, June 7 — Female hiker Jaslinda Saludin, 49, who was found safe yesterday after going missing for 14 days at Gunung Batu Putih, remains in stable condition but requires further treatment and monitoring at Tapah Hospital.
Perak Health Director Datuk Dr Feisul Idzwan Mustapha said Jaslinda underwent a computed tomography (CT) scan at Teluk Intan Hospital early this morning as part of efforts to assess her condition.
“Initial examinations found that the victim did not suffer any serious injuries. However, she still requires close monitoring and several additional examinations to ensure her recovery progresses well,” he told Bernama when contacted today.
He said the duration of her hospital stay would depend on her recovery progress and the outcome of further medical assessments.
Jaslinda was found in a weak condition near Kampung Lubuk Gaharu, Pos Musoh, at about 5pm yesterday by a resident of the village.
She was reported missing while taking part in the Trans Spencer Chapman hiking expedition on May 23 along the Pos Gedung–Gunung Bah Gading–Gunung Batu Putih–Kuala Woh route with 13 other participants and two Forestry Mountain Guides. — Bernama

Title: Flood control, water security in focus as Malaysia and Thailand step up Sungai Golok cooperation
Article:
KOTA BHARU, June 7 — The Thai Cabinet’s approval of the Sungai Golok Basin development project opens up avenues for closer cooperation between Malaysia and Thailand in addressing flood risks, water security and ecosystem restoration in the border areas of both countries.
National Security Council (NSC) Director-General Datuk Raja Nushirwan Zainal Abidin said Malaysia also intends to implement related developments in the area, though the matter is still in the planning stages.
He added that the government will continue to communicate with Thailand to ensure that any development planning involving the Sungai Golok Basin can be coordinated, given that it involves the interests of both nations.
“For that purpose, we will constantly remain in contact with the Thai government because it involves the interests of both countries,” he told reporters after an NSC meeting at the Kota Darulnaim Complex here today.
The Thai Cabinet was previously reported to have approved a joint environmental project with Malaysia aimed at reducing flood risks, enhancing water security and restoring the ecosystem in the Sungai Golok Basin.
The initiative has received US$4 million (RM15.9 million) in funding from the Global Environment Facility (GEF) and will be implemented over a 48-month period with the support of the United Nations’ Food and Agriculture Organisation (FAO).
The project was jointly developed by Thailand’s Office of National Water Resources and Malaysia’s Department of Irrigation and Drainage to strengthen transboundary water resource management.
According to Raja Nushirwan, the development of the Sungai Golok Basin is not only crucial for flood and environmental management but also carries implications for the well-being of residents and the development of border areas in both countries.
The project also encompasses the development of a shared database, flood and drought risk assessments, as well as the preparation of a joint strategic action plan for the river basin.
Meanwhile, he added that the government is also ironing out the details of economic initiatives to assist communities affected by the demolition of 91 illegal jetties along Sungai Golok, including those in Rantau Panjang and Tumpat.
He said the initiative is still in the detailing phase and will be implemented once coordination between the federal and state governments is finalised. — Bernama

Title: You can’t park there — Woman crashes Porsche into Gurney Drive hotel after family visit
Article:
GEORGE TOWN, June 7 — An elderly woman accidentally stepped on the accelerator, causing the Porsche she was driving to crash into the lobby area of a hotel at Persiaran Gurney here today, an incident that has also gone viral on social media.
Northeast District Police Chief ACP Abdul Rozak Muhammad said that the police received a report regarding the incident from the 61-year-old local woman at 12:05 pm and investigations revealed that the accident occurred at around 11:15 am.
“The incident happened as the senior citizen was preparing to head home after visiting family members. No casualties were reported, and the estimated losses are still under investigation,” he said in a statement today.
He added that the case is being investigated under Section 43 of the Road Transport Act 1987.
Earlier, several photos went viral showing a grey Porsche inside a hotel lobby, believed to have lost control before crashing into the area. — Bernama
